title: Paneer Butter Masala

ingredients.title: For the Gravy
items: 1 tbsp oil (optional, to prevent butter from burning)
1/4 cup cashew nuts, soaked in warm water for 15 minutes
1 tsp red chili powder (adjust to taste)

instruction.title: Instructions
steps: Prepare Cashew Paste: Drain the soaked cashews and blend with 2–3 tbsp water to make a smooth paste. Set aside.
Sauté Onions and Spices: Heat butter and oil in a heavy-bottom pan over medium heat.
Add cumin seeds and let them splutter.
Add finely chopped onions and sauté until golden brown.
Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for 1–2 minutes until aromatic.
Tomato Base: Add pureed tomatoes and cook until the oil separates (about 8–10 minutes).
Stir in red chili powder, turmeric, coriander powder, and salt. Cook for another 2–3 minutes.
Make Gravy: Add cashew paste and mix well. Cook for 3–4 minutes.
Add water to adjust consistency and bring the gravy to a gentle simmer.
Stir in sugar (if using) and garam masala.
Cook Paneer: In a separate pan, lightly sauté paneer cubes in 1 tbsp butter until golden (optional, for extra flavor).
Add paneer to the gravy and simmer for 5 minutes.
Stir in fresh cream and cook for another 2 minutes on low heat.
Garnish and Serve: Garnish with a swirl of cream and chopped coriander leaves.
Serve hot with naan, roti, or steamed basmati rice.

chefTips: For a smoother gravy, strain the tomato-cashew mixture before cooking further.
Use fresh, soft paneer for the best texture.
Adjust butter and cream to make it richer or lighter as per taste.
